hawkish_phrases: inflation is projected to rise to close to the target in the near term
CPI inflation rises temporarily above the 2% target towards the end of 2021
a temporary period of excess demand
Spare capacity is eliminated as activity picks up during 2021
inflation returns to around 2% in the medium term
dovish_phrases: The Committee does not intend to tighten monetary policy at least until there is clear evidence that significant progress is being made in eliminating spare capacity
there is judged to be spare capacity in the economy at present
it is likely that labour market slack has remained higher than implied by this measure
will take whatever action is necessary to achieve its remit
These transitory developments should have few direct implications for inflation over the medium term
summary: The MPC unanimously voted to maintain Bank Rate at 0.1% and asset purchases at £895 billion, with explicit forward guidance that it does not intend to tighten policy until there is clear evidence of eliminating spare capacity and achieving the 2% inflation target sustainably.
